title: Perform Bag Out to Clear Remaining Totes at End of Sort
summary: Use bag out at the end of a sort to clear remaining items from the sorter system before the next sort configuration begins. The source describes bag out as closing all chutes, ensuring totes with items are exchanged or marked for exchange, and clearing the system back to front until everything is out of the system.
when_to_use: Use when the current sort is complete and the system needs to be cleared so it is ready for the next sort configuration.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to define undocumented criteria for when bag out starts.
Do not use this runbook to define how tote exchanges are executed beyond what the source states.
Do not use this runbook as a detailed HMI control procedure; the source does not provide exact UI controls.

responsibilities: Confirm the sort is done before initiating bag out.
Ensure totes with items are exchanged or marked for exchange.
Monitor progress until everything is out of the system.

safety_notes: Use only the source-supported bag out sequence; do not add unsupported controls or criteria.
The source does not provide lockout, maintenance isolation, or physical hazard controls for this activity.
access_or_tools_needed: Visibility into tote contents or exchange marking status
Awareness of zone layout during back-to-front clearing

access_required: Operator access sufficient to close chutes
Operator access sufficient to start bag out
Visibility into tote contents or exchange marking status
system_preconditions: The system needs to be cleared before the next sort configuration begins.

visual_references.description: Sorter layout and zone-related training frame referenced for bag out flow, zone behavior, and farthest-area/back-to-front clearing concepts.

steps.title: Confirm bag out is needed
steps.purpose: Ensure bag out is only started at the end of a sort when the system must be cleared for the next sort.
steps.instruction: Confirm that the current sort is done and that the system needs to be cleared before the next sort configuration begins.
steps.expected_result: Bag out is recognized as the correct end-of-sort activity.
steps.healthy_condition: The sort is complete and the system is being transitioned for the next sort.
steps.failure_condition: Bag out is attempted without confirmation that the sort is done or without a need to clear the system for the next sort.

actions.label: Confirm end-of-sort condition
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the sort is done and the system needs to be cleared before the next sort configuration begins.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Lifecycle reference showing bag-out as part of the sort lifecycle before preparing for the next sort.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if it is not confirmed that the current sort is complete.
Escalate if the transition point between sorts is unclear from current operations.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Bag out works when you're done with the sort and need to bag out all locations.

source_ref.quote_or_summary: Bag out works when you're done with the sort and need to bag out all locations... everything has to come out of the system... all chutes are closed... any tote with an item in it gets an exchange or marked for an exchange... in bag out it's just back to front... once completed, everything is out of the system and they're ready to start another sort.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Bag out is part of the lifecycle of a sort and is used for locations with remaining items in totes before returning toward the next operating state.

steps.title: Close all chutes
steps.purpose: Place all sorter destinations into the bag-out condition described by the source.
steps.instruction: Close every chute, whether it has anything in it or not.
steps.expected_result: All chutes are closed for bag out.
steps.healthy_condition: Every chute is closed across the sorter.
steps.failure_condition: Any chute remains open during bag out.

actions.label: Close all chutes
actions.exact_action: Close every chute, whether it has anything in it or not.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System state page reference showing bag out as a system action.
Bag out control reference stating system bag out closes all destinations.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if all chutes cannot be closed.
Escalate if chute state cannot be confirmed.

source_ref.quote_or_summary: Bag out works when you're done with the sort and need to bag out all locations... everything has to come out of the system... all chutes are closed... any tote with an item in it gets an exchange or marked for an exchange... in bag out it's just back to front... once completed, everything is out of the system and they're ready to start another sort.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: System bag out will close all destinations.

steps.title: Start bag out
steps.purpose: Initiate the system state used to clear remaining items from the sorter.
steps.instruction: Start bag out using the available system bag out control.
steps.expected_result: The system enters bag out / close out behavior.
steps.healthy_condition: Bag out is active and the system begins clearing remaining totes with items.
steps.failure_condition: Bag out does not start or the system does not transition into bag out behavior.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System page showing System Bag Out as an available action.
Overall System Bag Out Control slide.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the bag out control is unavailable.
Escalate if the system does not enter bag out after operator initiation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The system page shows system state actions including System Bag Out.
Bag out or close out closes chutes and exchanges totes with items.

steps.title: Ensure totes with items are exchanged or marked for exchange
steps.purpose: Make sure every tote that still contains an item is included in the bag-out clearing process.
steps.instruction: Identify any tote that still has an item in it and ensure it gets an exchange or is marked for an exchange.
steps.expected_result: All totes containing items are queued for exchange or marked for exchange as part of bag out.
steps.healthy_condition: No tote with remaining items is left unaddressed.
steps.failure_condition: A tote with items is not exchanged and is not marked for exchange.

actions.label: Identify totes with items
actions.exact_action: Identify any tote that still has an item in it.

actions.label: Exchange or mark for exchange
actions.exact_action: Ensure it gets an exchange or is marked for an exchange.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Exchange process reference for understanding tote exchange behavior.
Rack/container lookup visual states that can help identify tote presence in rack locations.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if tote contents or exchange marking status cannot be determined.
Escalate if totes with items remain but are not being exchanged.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Any tote with an item in it gets an exchange or marked for an exchange.

source_ref.quote_or_summary: Bag out works when you're done with the sort and need to bag out all locations... everything has to come out of the system... all chutes are closed... any tote with an item in it gets an exchange or marked for an exchange... in bag out it's just back to front... once completed, everything is out of the system and they're ready to start another sort.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: System bag out will close all destinations and exchange all totes with parcels in them.

steps.title: Follow back-to-front bag out sequencing
steps.purpose: Apply the bag-out travel pattern described by the source instead of normal exchange priority logic.
steps.instruction: Move through the bag out sequence from back to front rather than by normal priority and time.
steps.expected_result: Bag out clearing follows back-to-front sequencing.
steps.healthy_condition: Bag out progresses back to front with reduced travel compared with normal priority/time exchange behavior.
steps.failure_condition: The system or operation continues using normal priority/time exchange behavior instead of back-to-front bag out behavior.

actions.label: Verify bag-out sequence logic
actions.exact_action: Move through the bag out sequence from back to front rather than by normal priority and time.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Sorter layout and zone arrangement supporting the back-to-front clearing explanation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if bag out does not appear to be progressing back to front.
Escalate if normal priority/time behavior appears to continue during bag out.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In bag out it's just back to front.

source_ref.quote_or_summary: Bag out works when you're done with the sort and need to bag out all locations... everything has to come out of the system... all chutes are closed... any tote with an item in it gets an exchange or marked for an exchange... in bag out it's just back to front... once completed, everything is out of the system and they're ready to start another sort.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In normal exchanges it's based on priority and time and in bag out it's just back to front so that there's less travel time.

source_ref.quote_or_summary: All the AGVs go to the farthest area of their zone... then it just goes every other... in normal exchanges it's based on priority and time and in bag out it's just back to front so that there's less travel time.

steps.title: Observe AGV farthest-area zone behavior
steps.purpose: Understand and monitor the zone-based AGV movement pattern used during bag out.
steps.instruction: Note that AGVs are described as going to the farthest area of their zone first and then proceeding through the remaining locations.
steps.expected_result: AGV movement aligns with the source-described farthest-area-first behavior within each zone.
steps.healthy_condition: AGVs go to the farthest area of their zone first and then continue through remaining locations in that zone.
steps.failure_condition: AGV movement does not align with the described zone-based farthest-area-first bag-out pattern.

actions.label: Observe AGV zone progression
actions.exact_action: Note that AGVs are described as going to the farthest area of their zone first and then proceeding through the remaining locations.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Layout and zone-related training image for farthest-area positioning and zone progression.
Facility layout context showing connected operating areas relevant to AGV movement.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if AGV movement appears inconsistent with zone-based bag-out behavior.
Escalate if zone layout awareness is insufficient to verify farthest-area-first progression.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: All the AGVs go to the farthest area of their zone... then it just goes every other.

source_ref.quote_or_summary: All the AGVs go to the farthest area of their zone... then it just goes every other... in normal exchanges it's based on priority and time and in bag out it's just back to front so that there's less travel time.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: An AGV in a zone only picks up and drops off in its zone.

source_ref.quote_or_summary: The sorter side has zones... an AGV in a zone only picks up and drops off in its zone... comes into its zone, sees if anything needs to be exchanged, moves on... it's basically FIFO.

steps.title: Continue until the system is empty
steps.purpose: Complete bag out so no remaining items are left in the system before the next sort.
steps.instruction: Continue until everything is out of the system.
steps.expected_result: All remaining items are removed from the system and the sorter is ready to start another sort.
steps.healthy_condition: Everything is out of the system and the sorter is ready to start another sort.
steps.failure_condition: Items remain in the system after bag out is completed.

actions.label: Verify system is cleared
actions.exact_action: Continue until everything is out of the system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Lifecycle reference showing bag-out completion before the next sort cycle.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if items remain in the system after bag out is completed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Everything has to come out of the system... once completed, everything is out of the system and they're ready to start another sort.

source_ref.quote_or_summary: Bag out works when you're done with the sort and need to bag out all locations... everything has to come out of the system... all chutes are closed... any tote with an item in it gets an exchange or marked for an exchange... in bag out it's just back to front... once completed, everything is out of the system and they're ready to start another sort.

success_criteria: All chutes have been closed for bag out.
All totes with items have been exchanged or marked for exchange.
Bag out has progressed using the back-to-front clearing pattern described by the source.
Everything is out of the system.
The sorter is ready to start another sort.
healthy_conditions: The sort is complete before bag out begins.
Totes with items are addressed through exchange or exchange marking.
AGVs clear from the farthest area of their zone first and proceed through remaining locations.
The system is empty at completion.
failure_conditions: Bag out is started without confirming the sort is done.
Any chute remains open during bag out.
A tote with items is not exchanged or marked for exchange.
Bag out does not follow the source-described back-to-front behavior.
Items remain in the system after bag out is completed.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if items remain in the system after bag out is completed.
Escalate if bag out cannot be started or its state cannot be confirmed.
Escalate if chute closure cannot be completed or verified.
Escalate if tote contents or exchange marking status cannot be determined.
Escalate if AGV movement does not align with the described back-to-front, farthest-area-first zone behavior.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Sorter layout design and zone-related visual support for bag out movement.
System page showing bag out as a system state action.
Overall System Bag Out Control behavior.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Bag out works when you're done with the sort and need to bag out all locations... everything has to come out of the system... all chutes are closed... any tote with an item in it gets an exchange or marked for an exchange... in bag out it's just back to front... once completed, everything is out of the system and they're ready to start another sort.

All the AGVs go to the farthest area of their zone... then it just goes every other... in normal exchanges it's based on priority and time and in bag out it's just back to front so that there's less travel time.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact HMI button names, menu paths, or click-by-click bag out initiation steps.
The source does not provide a detailed method for determining tote contents or exchange marking status.
The source does not define exact completion indicators beyond everything being out of the system.
The source does not specify role handoffs or supporting-role responsibilities.
The source does not provide a time estimate for bag out completion.
The source does not provide commands, API calls, or service procedures for this activity.
